headlines.title: Japan's Nikkei recoups early losses as US signals end of Iran strikes
Japanese Yen moves little after reaching six-week lows
The Bank of Japan is set to hold its first-ever meeting without a governor, causing some market unease—not over fears of a rate hike, but over concerns of not understanding the messaging.
Japan's Nikkei recoups early losses as US signals end of Iran strikes
U.S.-Iran tensions flare up again, Asian stocks decline; Japanese equities fall 1.5%, while South Korean shares plunge as much as 4%.
U.S.-Iran tensions flare up again, Asian stocks decline; Japanese equities fall 1.5%, while South Korean shares plunge as much as 4%.
Japan's Nikkei recoups early losses as US signals end of Iran strikes

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 미국-이란 간 지정학적 긴장 고조로 장 초반 하락했으나, 긴장 완화 조짐이 나타나며 낙폭을 대부분 회복하는 변동성 큰 흐름을 보였습니다. 엔화는 6주래 최저치 기록 후 큰 움직임 없이 보합세를 유지했습니다. 한편, 일본은행(BOJ)이 총재 부재 상태로 사상 첫 회의를 개최할 예정이어서 시장의 이목이 쏠리고 있습니다. 이는 금리 인상 우려보다는 정책 메시지 전달의 불확실성에 대한 불안감을 키우는 요인으로 작용하고 있습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese stocks saw a volatile session, initially declining due to renewed US-Iran tensions but largely recovering early losses as signals of de-escalation emerged. While the Yen showed little movement after hitting six-week lows, market attention also focused on the Bank of Japan's upcoming unprecedented meeting without a governor. This situation is causing some unease, not over rate hike fears, but due to concerns about potentially unclear policy messaging.
